issues.title: Implement multi-branch mode
issues.dod: Issues and op log are stored on a separate `issues/` branch. Worker ops read/write to that branch without affecting the working branch. All existing single-branch tests continue to pass. A config flag or auto-detection switches between modes.
notes: Single-branch mode must remain fully functional as the default.
Consider how git worktrees or sparse checkout can isolate the issues branch.

issues.title: Branch isolation for worker operations
issues.dod: Worker commands (claim, note, heartbeat, transition) read and write exclusively to the issues branch when in multi-branch mode. No cross-contamination of working branch history.
notes: Must handle concurrent access from multiple workers on the same issues branch.

issues.title: Cross-branch merge detection and auto-transition
issues.dod: When a working branch is merged into the main branch, Trellis detects the merge and auto-transitions associated in-progress issues to `merged` state. Detection works via post-merge git hook or explicit `trls sync` command.
notes: In single-branch mode this is a no-op (done == merged). This only matters in multi-branch mode.

issues.title: PR-based done-to-merged workflow
issues.dod: Issues transition to `done` when work is complete and to `merged` only after the associated PR is merged. `trls status` shows issues awaiting merge separately from done issues. Supports both GitHub PRs and plain git merges.
notes: PR association can be recorded via `trls link-pr --issue ID --pr URL`.

issues.title: Multi-worker conflict resolution
issues.dod: When two workers attempt to claim the same issue simultaneously, exactly one succeeds. Concurrent op log appends are resolved without data loss using git's atomic commit semantics. Conflict detection covers claim, transition, and create ops.
notes: Use git's compare-and-swap (fetch + append + push with retry on conflict) as the concurrency primitive.
Exponential backoff on retry is required to avoid thundering herd.

issues.title: Worker presence and claim visibility
issues.dod: `trls workers` command lists all active workers, their current claimed issues, claim TTLs, and last heartbeat time. Stale workers (no heartbeat for 2x TTL) are shown as inactive.

issues.title: Audit log viewer command
issues.dod: `trls log [--issue ID] [--worker ID] [--since TIME]` displays op log entries in human-readable form with filtering. Output includes op type, issue, worker, timestamp, and relevant payload fields.
notes: JSON output mode (`--json`) should also be supported for scripting.

issues.title: Issue assignment and reassignment
issues.dod: `trls assign --issue ID --worker WORKER-ID` assigns an issue to a specific worker. Assigned issues appear first in that worker's `trls ready` output. `trls unassign --issue ID` removes the assignment. Assignment is recorded in the op log.
notes: Assignment is advisory, not exclusive — any worker can still claim an unblocked issue.

issues.title: TUI dashboard
issues.dod: `trls tui` launches an interactive terminal UI showing the issue board (columns: open, in-progress, done), active workers, and recent ops. Supports keyboard navigation and issue detail view. Built with bubbletea or equivalent.
notes: Read-only in first iteration; write operations (claim, transition) can be added later.

issues.title: GitHub Issues integration
issues.dod: `trls github-sync` bidirectionally syncs Trellis issues with GitHub Issues on the configured repository. Trellis issue state maps to GitHub issue open/closed state and labels. Sync is incremental and idempotent.
notes: Requires a GitHub token in config or environment.
Conflict resolution: Trellis op log is authoritative; GitHub is a mirror.

issues.title: Webhook support for external notifications
issues.dod: Trellis can POST op log events to a configured webhook URL when ops are appended. Payload follows a documented JSON schema. Supports per-event-type filtering. Webhook URL is stored in git config or `.trellis/config`.
notes: Webhook delivery is best-effort; no retry queue in first iteration.

issues.title: Issue templates and project scaffolding
issues.dod: `trls init --template NAME` scaffolds a new project with a starter set of issues loaded from a named template. Templates are YAML or JSON files stored in `.trellis/templates/`. A built-in `software-project` template is included.
notes: Templates are separate from plan files but use the same issue schema.
Community templates can be loaded from a URL.
